neighborhoods.name: Centro Historico
neighborhoods.description: Massive UNESCO colonial center with ornate churches, presidential palace, and dramatic Andean setting
neighborhoods.character: Grand colonial UNESCO, cultural, improving walkability

housing.predominantType: Restored colonial apartments and historic conversions

neighborhoods.character_notes: The historic and commercial heart of Quito. Most walkable area with best access to cultural attractions, restaurants, and public services.

neighborhoods.name: Cumbaya
neighborhoods.description: Eastern valley suburb with warmer climate, shopping malls, and university campuses
neighborhoods.character: Modern suburban, warmer valley, university area

housing.predominantType: Modern houses, condos, and gated communities

neighborhoods.character_notes: Established residential area offering a quieter lifestyle while maintaining reasonable access to Quito's center and services.

neighborhoods.name: Quitumbe / Sur de Quito
neighborhoods.description: Southern bus terminal area with affordable housing and improving infrastructure
neighborhoods.character_notes: More affordable option for budget-conscious retirees. May require a car or longer transit times to reach central amenities.

neighborhoods.name: La Floresta / Gonzalez Suarez
neighborhoods.description: Bohemian and upscale neighborhoods with craft breweries, art spaces, and international restaurants

housing.predominantType: Art Deco apartments, modern condos, and renovated houses

neighborhoods.character_notes: Area with established international community and expat-oriented services. English is more commonly spoken here than in other neighborhoods.
